name: Taliban Supreme Council
title: Supreme Leader and Shura Council of the Islamic Emirate of Afghanistan

compatibilityOverrides.reason: Durand Line disputes and cross-border militant tensions
China cautiously engages for Belt-and-Road mineral access
Sunni-Shia sectarian divide despite shared anti-Western stance
Historical Soviet-Afghan enmity tempered by pragmatic engagement

biography: The Taliban's collective leadership, nominally headed by Supreme Leader Hibatullah Akhundzada, seized control of Afghanistan in August 2021 after the US withdrawal. Ruling as a theocratic ISTJ-oriented hierarchy, they impose strict Sharia law, have effectively erased women from public life, and govern through a Shura council system that values tradition, obedience, and religious orthodoxy above all. Despite international isolation and a humanitarian crisis, they maintain iron grip through tribal networks, opium revenue, and the Haqqani network's security apparatus, while cautiously engaging China and Gulf states for economic survival.
